reviews.text: I will start by saying I love the ambiance of Kokomo. Staff here is always friendly and music is always great during service and after hours.Food took entirely too long to come out. 40 minutes for an appetizer is crazy...Once they arrived, they were all tasty. I wish I took photos of the food. We ordered the curry crab gyoza which was so flavorful and the ackee and saltfish shumai.The oxtail flatbread is very large and lots of meat. They don't skimp out.Biang biang Rasta noodles is served with oyster mushrooms (so good) and peppers. I added shrimp to mine. Very tasty and filling. But keep in mind that the noodles are not penne and they are I think fettuccineThey must do better on their serving time. The restaurant was crowded. But it would be nice to have the expectation set that there will be a long wait.

Went here for brunch with friends because the vibes looked great and reviews were solid. We had a reservation right when they opened, but they were running about 20 minutes late -- not the best start.Once seated though, the space is beautiful. We were in a sunlit room and it really set the mood. Our server was great, and we ordered family-style. Just a heads up, they have a separate "Sunday drunch" menu so options are a bit limited.For apps, we got the curry crab gyoza which was a very small portion (6/10) and the roasted mushroom truffle flatbread which was the highlight of the meal (8.5/10).For mains, we tried the biang biang Rasta noodles with salmon (6/10) and the sofrino roasted chicken (5/10). Both were a bit underwhelming.I've heard their drinks are great but we didn't try any. Food-wise, I was a little disappointed, but still had a great time catching up with friends.

Went for their Thursday night girl dinner and had a good time! Friendly staff, live dj, good food and cocktails. The restaurant is beautifully decorated and dimly lit.The special is pasta, salad, fries and a cocktail for 25 bucks! But add chicken or shrimp to the pasta for an additional up charge so more like 35-37 bucks depending on your choice. They have happy hour all day which is good if you want additional drinks.I had the chicken pasta which was delicious but could've been hotter in temperature. Like you can tell them make a bunch in advance and have them sit under a warmer. The coconut express martini was delicious and strong (they ran out of coffee beans though!) so I got another one! Salad was basic but the fries were delicious, so crispy and served with a spicy aioli on the side.

I went with a party of 9. They accommodated us in a nice way. Very friendly staff.The old mountain Sauvignon blanc bottle was a good choice. It was shared amongst four people for $60.Caribbean Spiced Lamb - very delicious. The waiter said it was four pieces and comes with an egg on top. In actuality, it only came in 3 pieces and no egg on top. I feel like it should've came in 4 pieces as stated. The waiter wasn't accurate with his detail.Biang Biang Rasta Noodles - we added salmon on one and shrimps on another. The noodles did not need any proteins! The salmon was tough and the shrimp did not go well with it. Order the noodles just by itself. It will taste better.Crispy Crab Rice - very good.Curry Crab Gyoza - sauce was amazing. We saved the sauce for our next meal.Shrimp Tempura Bao Bao - was okay. Two small buns.Yardie Shrimp Flatbread - it was alright. The greens didn't pair well with it.Jerk Infused Wings - nice and saucyStandard birthday vanilla ice cream desert - kind of them to bring out for the birthday lady.Street parking. Bathrooms are a stairway downstairs.Note: 20% gratuity automatically added for a party of 2 or more.

First time here and the food was good. The hosts and our waiter were very good and attentive. From the Lemon drop to the Plantini drink we're very good. I had the jerk wings with the sauce on the side and the Curry Crab Goyza which was pretty good. And for my main dish I had the pumpkin infused pancakes with chicken and my friend she ahd the coconut rice with oxtail gravy amd her drink was the Painkiller. She had a little upset stomach from mixing the drink with the crab goyza because of the creams but our waiter brought her a drink that immediately stopped whatever was going on. The only thing I did not like is the seating. I dont like to have a bunch of people all around me I just wished they had real booths for people. I would go back but be at the bar especially since the next time I would be by myself. Overall it's a must go for me.

Kokomo had the perfect vibe. Chill, grown, dim, and just the right energy. The music was on point, and the service was good and very personable.We ordered the oxtail, curry shrimp, a burger, and the koko pasta flatbread. Now I would never normally order a pasta flatbread because it feels comedically greedy, but I am so glad my friend let greed win. That flatbread was incredible and honestly worth the visit on its own.The oxtail was also really good and a personal favorite. Overall, I would definitely return, if only for that flatbread alone.

my boyfie and I have been wanting to try this place ever since we saw the lamb and oxtail flatbread!! it looked so goood. we came in on a weekday afternoon, total of 3 customers at the time. Place played music, definitely romantic and giving an occasion vibe. Staff was friendly! Food came out after 15 mins or so. We ordered:Lamb 5/10: Flavor was salty, and sweet which I liked! however the lamb didn't taste fresh, there is a slight sour aftertaste. the food was also COLD! which was disappointing but we pushed through.Oxtail flatbread 9/10: YESSS SOOO GOOOD!!! the oxtail flatbread is so yummy and cheesy! do you see that cheese pull???? it was me and my boys fav.The drinks were good!A staff member asked if she could take a picture, and I thought it was for their ig or something but it was for it to be printed, framed and bought! so yes please for the memories  Will probably come back just for the flatbread!

Good energy, good food, and a vibe that carries the night.Saturday night here is lively--Afro-Caribbean music, dim lighting, and a slightly romantic but still social atmosphere. The DJ definitely set the tone.Service was friendly and helpful, but you could tell they were a bit busy. Still, they stayed attentive.Food highlights:The callaloo with plantain chips--perfectly spiced, slightly acidic, and honestly the standout.Curry crab gyoza had great flavor, though a couple were a little overdone.Drinks were solid--balanced and well made.By 8 PM, a lot of entrées were sold out, which was disappointing but also says a lot."Come for the vibe, stay for the flavor--but get there early."The Rozzay Standard:  Worth it (with timing in mind)Best for: birthdays, group dinners, or a night out with good music and energy
Food and drinks were great, as usual! The music, service and vibes were all great too at the Saturday brunch. Can't wait to come back!

Oxtail flatbread was amazing! I was skeptical at first because I am used to traditional oxtails with rice and peas...but I loved this flatbread. The food, drinks, service, music, ambiance were all fantastic. I can't wait to go back again and highly recommend!!

Inside is nicely decorated. The design is stylish w tables and space. Dj has nice best going . Staff is great and attentive. They charge a 20% service fee for parties of 2 or more that's crazy!. The food is heavy, light on ingredients and so so overall. The crab gyoza has no taste of crab, was dry and overpowered w the curry flavor. The flatbread had little shrimp on it, was a bit soggy and though decent in flavor wouldn't order again. I want to go back and try the wings and oxtail or red snapper to see how they do with them but otherwise would go for drinks and beat!

For a Friday night the vibe was just right.Flavor, Music and Decor.Highly recommend making reservations.This was a spot I've been eyeing for a long time and I'm glad I was able to experience it this weekend.As you can see in the pictures we were able to enjoy a few great things on the menu.For drinks we had watermelon, sorrel lemonade (nonalcoholic).Their herbed fries were so good we ended up ordering twice, I believe it's that delicious pepper sauce, (just the right amount of scotch bonnet taste), they serve it with that makes them taste even better. The sautéed callaloo was different (in a good way) served with plantain chips and some type of crema. One of our friends also ordered the ackee and salt fish shumai for starters.For our mains, my friend loved her coconut curry lentils suggested by one of the staff members. Thank you! My other friend ordered the oxtail flatbread (which I had to have with the pepper sauce, of course) . I ordered the jerk chicken wings, sweet plantains and coconut rice (some of the best any of us have had).Overall the service was excellent, attentive, friendly and on our pace. They resolved certain issues we had with diligence. I will definitely be back to savor some more items one the menu.

I recently visited Kokomo after driving in from New Jersey, and overall, my experience was mixed.Unfortunately, the initial impression wasn't great--the hostess was not very welcoming and came across as somewhat passive aggressive, which set the tone for the start of the visit. There was also a noticeable delay with the food.That being said, the food itself was absolutely delicious and worth the wait once it arrived. I was, however, disappointed that I didn't get the chance to try the plantini.On a positive note, the ambiance was very pleasant and definitely one of the highlights of the restaurant.With some improvements in service, this could easily be a top-tier dining experience.

Kokomo had been on my list for a while, and I finally stopped by. The vibe is great with both indoor and outdoor seating -- definitely make a reservation if you're coming with a group.I tried the Crispy Chicken Bao Bun which I recommend, the Koko Pasta Flatbread with chicken - it is a perfect portion, even though it came out last, and the XO Noodles -- flavorful but not as impressive as expected. The Mac & Cheese was surprisingly light and tasty.Service was a bit confusing due to a change in servers, and prices are definitely on the higher side. We were so full by the end, we had to skip dessert. Solid spot overall -- I'd come back to try more.

This was my first time at Kokomo. I've been to the block party but never sat inside of the restaurant and ate. The restaurant was clean and decorated nicely. Our waiter's name was Matthew. He was very pleasant, attentive, and cute. My friends were making him so nervous but he did so well lol.We came on a Sunday and had to order from the "Sunday Drunch" menu. The French toast and fried chicken was so so good. The Mac and cheese was good but wayyyyy too cheesy for me. The jerk infused wings were good but they weren't cooked well enough for my liking. I also ordered a 3 drink mimosa bundle, which was very good.I'll return to Kokomo.

A Gourmet Caribbean Escape at Kokomo!I had the pleasure of dining at Kokomo early one evening, and honestly, I was floored. This isn't just your typical neighborhood spot--it is unbelievably awesome.Located right by the scenic Brooklyn waterfront, here is why this place is a standout:The Atmosphere: While you can't actually see the water from your table inside, the decor is so well-executed that you'll feel like you've been transported to a secluded, high-end Caribbean destination. It is beautiful and truly immersive.The Food: This is super-gourmet, fine Caribbean dining. The flavors are elevated and sophisticated. There is "fine dining" pricing to match, but for the level of quality you're getting, it is worth every penny.The Drinks: They absolutely do not disappoint. Perfectly balanced and high-quality.The Service: The wait staff was kind, courteous, and incredibly efficient from start to finish.I will be coming back here again, and again, and again. I'll be recommending Kokomo to anyone who will listen! If you want to experience Caribbean cuisine at its most refined, this is the spot.

It was a Wednesday and I wanted bottomless beverages and brunch in the middle of the week and not too many places do that...this one does.  Shout out to a fellow yelp elite NYer for hitting me with some recommendations. Also, black owned at the end of Black History Month is a win.  The bartender/server (yes, I'm a bar fly) was not feeling her best but her personality didn't represent that. I forget her name, even though she told me.  She was nice in that beautiful New York ruggedness, Bronx origins I believe.  I hope that she's there the next time I visit.  It was quiet on this Wednesday afternoon with only a few tables being served and I got a moment to speak with one of the owners. She was cool.  They were doing photos for the restaurant this afternoon.The food was good. It's rare to find good food with bottomless drinks.  The French toast was perfect.  I could've gone for more of it than chicken. The chicken was well flavored but slightly dry, my only complaint.  I was actually speaking with another server about that after she asked me for my honest review.  I was clearly very social during this visit probably because that carafe didn't stay upright often.  All in all, this was a good spot that I'll stop into again I'm sure.

I've had this place bookmarked for quite some time and decided to go for dinner. I did not have a reservation but was told I could sign up and wouldn't be more than 30 minute wait, which was accurate. I received a text when a table opened ip and was seated.Unfortunately, things sorta went downhill from there. The server came over after about ten minutes, but I was still waiting for my friend. Once she arrived, it took about 10-15 for him to return. We placed our order and then we waited over 90 minutes for our food. It took 90 just for our basic appetizers to come and while I got my calloo, my friend's appetizer was incorrect. They took about 25 minutes to bring her correct appetizer, which was cake and salt fish. She was perturbed, as was I, but she did enjoy her appetizer.While we sitting there frustrated a different server must have noticed something was wrong and we explained we had been waiting extremely long and she said she noticed we had been there awhile. She offered us a drink on the house to make up for it and went to get our food. I had the Salmon and that came with white rice. It was okay and we also had some macaroni cheese which was okay as well.We both had bread pudding for dessert which seemingly followed the same theme of just being okay. I did enjoy the drinks (Henny Colada) but the wait time really left a sour taste. I appreciate teed the other server coming to assist-- she further explained that he way too many tables assigned to him and was overwhelmed. However, that should never happen where it takes damn near two hours to eat.

Kokomo is a gorgeous spot right near the water in Brooklyn. I appreciate their creative cocktails and variety of vegetarian options.To drink, I enjoyed the We Limin' and the Henneycolada is a must try - be sure to stir it or you're gonna get a huge mouthful of henney lol.To eat, my favorite item was definitely the Koko Flatbread. The Rasta Balls, Tofu Buns, and Mac & Cheese were also tasty. I could've passed on the plantains - they weren't bad but were just kind of mid.Desserts were a tad underwhelming and very small.Great vibes and our waiter was great!

I've come here an embarrassing amount of times just for that oxtail flatbread! It's my absolute favorite thing on the menu, but there's so many great things to choose from. I've come here for restaurant week, Valentine's Day, and casual drops ins the food never disappoints. It is very pretty inside, the perfect occasion dinner spot. The curry crab gyoza was extraordinary and I'll be getting that again. I also really like their Rasta pasta dish. The drinks are always good and strong. I highly recommend Kokomo!!

This is my 3rd visit to Kokomo. However, it was my first time dining inside the restaurant. Kokomo is a very beautiful restaurant. The manager was extremely helpful and polite.My friend and I both enjoyed our sweet chili wings as our appetizer choice and menu choices from the Sunday Drunch menu.  My friend ordered sweet plantain pancakes which were amazing. I will definitely have to order that next time I visit on a Sunday.What I love about Kokomo is the consistency and knowledge that I know the food is always going to be delicious.

Saturday, 6/7/2025 - Went to Kokomo and had lunch. Address is 65 Kent Ave, Brooklyn, NY 11249 (Williamsburg - North Side). Nice restaurant. The food dishes we got were all good. Our waitress was professional and nice. Water tasted great. Restaurant looked clean. Utensils, plates, cups, table clean. Across from us was more tables with swing chairs (that can fit 2 people in the swing chairs). You can't swing on it of course. But I thought that was unique. They made sure to refill our water cups. Bathroom was down stairs and clean too. They do charge you gratuity if it's 2 or more people in your party. You'll see it in your receipt.

Believe it or not, we actually picked Kokomo based on all the positive Yelp reviews, and, I gotta say, it definitely held its own. The vibe is lively and the energy in the room makes it a great spot for a night out.The waiter was friendly (wish I remembered her name to shout her out). We started by splitting the jerk infused wings, which had a solid kick without being overpowering. For my main, I wasn't going to come to a West Indian spot and not have the oxtail. It was tender, rich, and fell right off the bone. We finished with the pumpkin cheesecake, which was a really nice way to end the meal.The drinks were also a highlight. I switched between the Painkiller (classic Caribbean rum vibes, very smooth) and the Berry and Bright, which was a nice contrast with the whiskey and blackberry.Will definitely return. It's a vibe!

The vibe the service everything was perfect upon entry. Came here as a party of 10 for my friends bday dinner this was my first time here and I had this place bookmarked for a while years to be exact lol.... Now on to food since we were a large party we had a prefixed menu option which made the most sense and was worth it in my opinion! We had the infused wings, crab gyoza, and the shrimp for appetizers; the salad with the shrimp was everybody's fave I wasn't a fan of the wings and the gyoza was overpowered by the curry that you didn't taste crab however they were still decent. For entrees we had oxtails, salmon and the, vegetarian pasta everyone enjoyed their food I liked the tamarind sauce that came with the salmon it elevated the coconut rice for me. I do feel like the food could've been slightly better but it wasn't bad! The French75's were made to perfection as well! I want to come back in the warmer months to enjoy the rooftop.

I've been following the restaurant on Instagram for some time but after seeing some BTS videos of Sous Chef AJ, General Manager Tasia & CoOwner Kev -- I jumped out of bed, made a last minute resy and commuted two hours from Montclair to Brooklyn to try Kokomo for the first time!I was greeted with a huge smile by the host who checked me in, made me aware that my table was ready, and offered me a seat at the bar until the other 2 people in my party arrived. The smell of Caribbean food filled the air, the music was booming and vibes were great -- this Haitian kid felt at home.Once my siblings checked in about 10 minutes later, our host directed us to our table. As we were taking our coats off to sit, Tasia (I recognized her from Instagram) approached our table and made us aware that the kitchen was closing within 30 minutes. We thanked her and took our seats. No less than 60 seconds later, a gentleman sporting a wrinkled black polo came over, introduced himself as our waiter and echoed the general manager's message.I asked the waiter for his name and he shared that it was Sinque (forgive the spelling). He went on to share what items they were out of for the day. We asked for his recommendations and requested two minutes for us to review the menu. Sinque obliged. Feeling extremely rushed, we skimmed the menu, identified a few things to eat and waited for our waiter to return. When he did, we ordered:Appetizer:Oxtail flatbreadEntree:Pikliz Fish sandwichJerkmi burgerKoko's Chicken & wafflesSides:Sweet plantainsDessert:Rum raisin banana bread puddingDrinksPear & Ginger MimosaGinger AleGinger BeerThe oxtail flatbread was delicious! We really enjoyed the tender oxtails contrasted with the crispy onion on top. We sipped our drinks and made light conversation until our entrées arrived.My brother devoured his pikliz fish sandwich in a heartbeat but my sister was underwhelmed by her chicken and waffle dish. Her chicken looked amazing but tasted burnt (old oil?) but even worse - the waffle was literally inedible. My burger however was the freshest burger I've had in my life. The bread pudding was a bit small but packed with flavor. Given how dense the dessert was, the plastic spoons made it really challenging to dig in with ease. Food overall: 7/10.Having worked in hospitality for over 15 years (fast casual, fine dining & high volume hotels) I was really impressed by Kokomo's social media presence. The training videos and the standard of excellence clearly articulated by Kev, Tasia and others set the bar really high. That standard made me jump out of bed and take four trains for a two hour commute from NJ to Williamsburg. This is why I was extremely disappointed we didn't experience this level of excellence yesterday. Overall experience was sadly a 4/10. I will give it some time but will certainly be back and hope to have a better time.

This place is on the upscale side but everything here is delicious from their craft cocktails to their food! It was also the first time I sat in a swing for a restaurant. My friend and I shared the jerk chicken wings, braised oxtail and curry snapper with plantains and rice + beans. I had the soursop cocktail which if you've never had soursop, what are you doing? Get it right now!

I treated my friend to Kokomo for her graduation as we both had been wanting to try their brunch for a while. They did not disappoint, the ambiance is nice and lively but not too loud. The food was so good. I had the French Toast and Chicken and my friend had the Koko Pasta Flatbread with Jerk Chicken. Both dishes were filling and tasty. The rum syrup/sauce that came with was so good. The drinks are also good and strong. I enjoyed the Sop Thyme drink, it was very refreshing. I will more than likely come back to try their dinner menu or explore more of the brunch menu. The staff was also friendly and our orders came out In a timely manner.

Got the Carribean bowl sonce I wanted to try things i havent had before like callaloo and ackee and saltfish. Havent really had Carribean food before so not sure if it's supposed to be like that but the boiled dumpling was super hard and flavorless. The whole dish was pretty flavorless except for the ackee and saltfish which was quite yummy. But $35 seems overpriced. My friends got other dishes like the chicken and waffles which they enjoyed. If you want to do the bottomless brunch option then your whole table needs to get it so we opted not to. Service was nice tho since they were an hour late getting one of our dishes to us, they took it off our bill

Food was ammmmmmmaaaaaaazzzzzziiiiinnnnggggg. Ambiance was beautiful. Drinks were creepers( it will creep up on you) but delicious and very well crafted. Waiter was very attentive. I booked for a ladies brunch out and was not disappointed what so ever. Will be booking a private party for my birthday. A must try.

I've been here before, but this time was terrible. We came the day after thanksgiving for brunch. I opted for the bottomless option. The mimosas were watered down and the waiter was slow. Without warning, he told us that the bartender was leaving so I wouldn't be able to order mimosas anymore. I said could I at least get a few mimosas and he told me he could only give me 2. If they knew the bartender was leaving, I should've at least gotten a carafe.My food almost took forever. Thank God we got apps. We got the jerk pasta balls. When my food finally came out after an hour, it was just okay. I got the chicken & French toast. The French toast was tasty and sweet, but it was barely hot. We told our waiter about our experience and he didn't offer us anything. I think this will be my last time at Kokomo.

Yes, I absolutely recommend this restaurant for a number of reasons. They have excellent service and with our reservation on a Friday night, our party of 8 was seated promptly. Our waiter was very patient and explained menu items thoroughly.The food was tasty and delicious including the meals and appetizers. We especially liked the Ackee & Saltfish Stuffed Plantain Cups and oxtail flatbread.Overall the vibe was nice and the crowd was a mix of couples, groups, friends and family alike. This is a great place for celebrations of all kinds.

We had a great dinner here last night!  4 of us tried to slide into bar seats at Laser Wolf at 5:30, and missed the very small window of where that's possible.  I always knew Kfar was here and a good backup, so happy we finally "had the chance" to try it.We got several of their mezze - we loved it all, my personal favorites were the "Baklava" (kind of like a version of Halloumi saganaki?) and the kibbe nayah.  After 4 apps, 4 of us split the "grilled meats for 2", and in total it was about the perfect amount of food.The vibe is very cool and trendy, the service was fantastic, and food was every bit as good as Laser Wolf.  This place deserves to be a destination!

Stopped by K'far for drinks and the vibe was really cozy and effortlessly cool.The My Boy Blue (rye, plum, blueberry, lemon, honey, egg white) was very tasty! Deep purple, silky foam on top and tasted just as good as it looked. Smooth, fruity and perfectly balanced. Had it alongside a glass of Cava which was the perfect pairing. Solid drinks, good energy.

Went to K'Far Cafe before an improv show for tea and babka. Both were excellent -- the babka wasn't too sweet, just perfectly balanced. We had our eye on the pistachio sticky bun, but they were sold out. While sipping tea at the bar, I noticed they also have kunafe on the menu, which I didn't have time to try -- guess that just means another visit.About $24 total, which felt a bit pricey, but the quality was there. Great quick treat spot. We saw a lot of people working on their laptops.

Absolutely gorgeous interior, probably worth it just for the light and plant-filled dining room/patio, especially in the summer. The meal was yummy--crispy mushroom hummus, fluffy pita, fresh Arabic salad, a dynamic sabich platter with beautifully light almost tempura fried eggplant. The Yemeni latte was perfectly spiced and only a little sweet, highly recommend pairing it with your meal. I also tried some of the shakshuka, which was tasty, standard fare. The staff were very personable and helpful. I was looking for just a little more flavor out of each dish--a little note of citrus in the salad, some spice in the sabich sauce to complement the pickled veggies, but was still very happy with the meal. Great spot for a special occasion brunch.

Honestly had a very underwhelming experience at this restaurant. The highlight of the meal was the appetizers- the lamb tartar was really good but it was extremely greasy and did not have enough to cut on the oil and grease.I was not a fan of the baba ganoush and the smoked hamachi crudo. The hamachi was extremely fishy and did not taste fresh at all. The lamb could have had more flavor, and the brick chicken was dry.the ambiance was really nice though with the dim lighting!The apple olive oil cake was pretty dense as well. It was too dense and not light enough- what we thought an olive oil cake should taste like.The service was great though. Overall it was decent, was not too impressed for the price. Would not come again.

A truly great experience here, would highly recommend for folks looking for a great mediterranean/Israeli meal, because K'far delivers on everything.You must have a reservation first and foremost, so make one, because this place gets packed and stays packed.The ambiance is very dark and intimate, crowd is lively, food is fresh and flavorful, and I have ratings on what we loved and what we thought was just okay.Zuchinni App- Just Okay, the Labne was delicious though.Mushroom Hummus- Also pretty good, matched with the FRESH PITA, which is outrageously good, it's nice.The Meat Platter for 2- Incredible, wide variety to choose from, the Chicken is amazing, the steak is juicy and fresh, highly recommend to get this for the table.With drinks, food, apps and all in it was about $250, so decently priced as well. Very accommodating staff, friendly, easy to talk to.5 stars. Loved it.

Visited the K'Far Philly location last year and was so excited to come back to the Williamsburg spot (located on the ground floor of the Huxton hotel). Came on Sunday morning around 10AM for brunch and it was suprisingly not too busy so we were seated quickly. The ambiance is airy and open with lots of natural light.- Jerusalem Bagel Egg and Cheese (5/5) - Similar, if not the same as the Simit sandwich from K'Far Philly? The bagel is long and thin, and the egg and cheese are perfect with the spicy green sauce.- Kubaneh Toast with Avocado (4/5) - the labneh is tart and balanced well by the creamy avocado- Pistachio Sticky Bun (5/5) - Soft, sweet and so delicous. Just as a good as I remember from Philly.- Yemenite Iced Coffee (5/5) - Delicously spicedBig fan of K'far and will definitely be returning.

I stopped in for breakfast and tried two items.Jerusalem bagel - crispy on the outside and chewy on the inside, but not as chewy as a standard New York bagel. The inside is insanely rich - creamy cheese and eggs. It's a huge portion, but maybe a bit hard to justify paying $17 for.Pistachio bun - really delicious. The bun is fluffy, and the brown butter glaze on the outside is crispy and insanely flavorful. The pistachios on top complete the picture. Tastes delicious warm, and totally worth the $6.I think this spot works better as a place to hang out and grab a pastry, instead of having a full meal here.

This restaurant is situated in the lobby of The Hoxton Hotel in an open area. There's a bar on one side and a cafe in another while also serving a restaurant menu. Most people here are working on their laptops as did I. It was pretty packed and started clearing up around 5pm when the work day is done for most.I ordered a Matcha Latte ($6.50) at the cafe counter and it was ready in minutes. The matcha is nice without sweetener, but you can add. I was able to snag a table on the side, which was the perfect spot to work. Some of the tables near the wall and shelves have outlets if you need. They also have free wifi that was super fast. Last but not least, the ambiance, decor, and vibes is so airy, modern, and natural that just puts you in a good mood. It's such a great working environment; no wonder everyone comes here to work. I honestly wish I was closer to here!

The pastries are excellent, especially the pistachio sticky bun, and the savory stuff like the mushroom hummus is just as good. Everything feels bright, flavorful, and well done.Great vibe, consistently solid food, and an easy place to come back to whether it's for brunch or a casual meal.

Wow wow wow-- amazing coffee and pastries! I loved the Yemeni latte; it was warmly spiced and so flavorful. I tried two burekas during my time there-- the feta scallion which is supposedly their best seller-- absolutely delicious. The last morning I was there I tried the potato one-- WOW. It was so nicely spiced, simple but flavorful. I'd absolutely get it again. The service is nice and the people are kind. It's situated in the hotel lobby so lots of seating and people coming to and fro. Love it!

This is a review for the lobby portion of K'far - we really enjoy ordering coffee and working in the lobby area of the Hoxton. Today we ordered the shakshuka and it came with pita so it was perfect for two people!!

K'Far BK is such a vibe!Location: Williamsburg, BrooklynHighly recommendMy partner and I originally planned to hit the rooftop at The Hoxton in Williamsburg, but with a 2hour wait, we pivoted and honestly, we're glad we did. K'Far BK love love love.We grabbed drinks at the bar and were lucky to be served by two super friendly and cool bartenders. The ambiance was beautiful, great lighting, relaxed but stylish atmosphere, and just a really inviting space to hang out.The drinks was so good. I had an espresso martini especially since they have a coffee bar, so I knew it'd be top tier), and it did not disappoint. I also went for a G&T with Hendrick's, which was refreshing and hit the spot. My partner went all in with an extra dirty gin martini made with Bombay Sapphire.Forgot to mention the Persian Margarita  so good!Our friend ordered the Chin Chin Spirit, and I managed to sneak a sip, it was amazing. Definitely something I'd try for myself next time.We didn't get to order food this time around, but the menu looked like they had some great options. Already planning a return visit, and fingers crossed we'll make it up to that rooftop next time.

Everything we had from the scallion & feta Boreka, to the kale salad , to the eggs and merguez, to the smoked salmon and egg & cheese Jerusalem bagels were 5 stars but as you can tell by my face the sticky pistachio bun and the almond hallah danish were something to contemplate.Nothing shorty of divinity in the form of baked goods.I don't always have a religious experience eating brunch but this Sunday I did.K'far is a gem that more people should know about.

We had a delicious brunch at K'far. The fan favorites included the pancakes, the Jerusalem bagels, and the arayes, and the tehina ceasar. The service was good, no complaints there. I really love the ambiance of the restaurant. There is a lot of greenery, and part of the restaurant looks like an atrium or greenhouse. It's really pretty. I would definitely come back again.

This is my second time here.  1st time was just at the bar and the drinks were perfectly balanced.  This time was for brunch and everything was delicious. Don't skip out on the pistachio sticky bun which was amazing!!

I have never been to K'far....what a beautful space, great space to dine with a group of friends. The food was KILLER. The brick chicken....FAVORITE as well as the Hummus with Mushrooms.  Zoe was AND AMAZING server while Sam greeted us with a kind smile as she poured the water (dream team). Looking forward to dining there again!!!

I came here with a friend for brunch and we absolutely loved it. Food we got:- burrata toast with apricot - one of the most uniquely delicious things i've ever had. both of us considered this our favorite on the menu- shakshuka - this shakshuka was unique bc the eggs were very smoky. i liked it a lot- pistachio sticky bun - delicious, not too sweet, and super fluffy- crispy mushroom hummus - the mushrooms weren't really crispy but the hummus was still good- yemenite latte - this was good but it wasn't really uniqueThe inside of K'Far is absolutely gorgeous - great lighting, decor, and lots of plants. The staff were attentive and patient. i will go back!

I've been to K'far a few times for brunch, but only recently tried dinner. I'm a huge fan of Laserwolf and K'far is the sister restaurant on the lower floor of the Hoxton in Williamsburg. Its a great space and nice ambiance for brunch as well as dinner. I went with a friend and we started with the crispy mushroom hummus, very good and creamy with chunks of mushroom. For our main, we shared the mixed grill for two. Everything was excellent: cooked to perfection, flavorful, with just the right amount of spice. The meats are served on a thin pita, perfect for making mini-wraps, and come with mango chutney and other pairings that complement the dish beautifully. The quality and flavors are on par with Laserwolf, at a similar price point, but reservations here are much easier to get. Highly recommend both the mixed grill and K'far overall.

The food was overall very good. My favorite was the apricot toast with burrata - tasted unlike anything i've had before and really enjoyed the complex sweet savory flavors.I also enjoyed the mushroom hummus, though I wish the mushrooms were crispy.The shakshuka was very smokey and unlike anything I've i've had. It was very rich so I wouldn't want to eat a lot of it. I'd probably pass on this again but glad I tried.The pistachio sticky bun was also good. Not too sweet.The inside is absolutely beautiful and well lit. Great service as well

First of all the service is excellent and the restaurant beautiful. The dinner menu has changed since the last time I was there and is extremely restrictive.The hummus,as always was great and the food was good to excellent but the chef was heavy handed with the hot pepper on almost all the main courses. I would like to think the chef was having a bad night.  I would go back although considering upstairs the next time.

Went to Kfar for brunch on Memorial Day and didn't realize it was in the Hoxton but it is a great space! We split the pistachio bun, which was worth all of the calories, I had the seasonal spritz to drink and the Jerusalem Bagel with smoked salmon. It was delicious. The staff was super accommodating and gave me no issues with my service dog. I would go back to try dinner service but I love that they do breakfast, lunch and dinner since it's in the hotel, so a solid choice in Williamsburg.

I adore K'Far's brunch. The smoked salmon bagel on a Jerusalem style bagel is sooooo good and all of their pastries are *chef's kiss* fantastic. I particularly loved the babka, pistachio sticky bun, & potato boreka.The indoor patio vibe is also really nice with the high glass ceilings.

I came here for a Sunday brunch at 1:30PM and didn't really have an issue snagging a reservation the day before! My friend and I shared the crispy mushroom hummus (9/10 really good but wasn't sure what was supposed to be crispy about it) and burrata apricot kubaneh (10/10 as recommended by our waiter and was probably my favorite dish). My friend then ordered the French toast which she said she enjoyed and I got the smoked trout kubaneh (7/10 the apricot one was way better and this just needed a little something extra added to it and the ratio was heavy on the bread)We had a very friendly waiter and the interior was nicely decorated! I'd definitely come back!

Absolutely obsessed - I visited here with some friends for brunch and was blown away by how incredible the food was!We started off rocky being seated 20 minutes after our reservation time - and we had come 10 minutes early, but after we were seating things started looking up.We all ordered tea/coffee, which was nothing to write home about, but standard.Our entrees is where we were really impressed - we ordered the avocado toast, the pita French toast, Caesar salad with shawarma on the side, and the shakshouka.I cannot explain to you how I was mind blown by the pita French toast, I was fully expecting a flat French toast but they stacked pitas on top of each other and created this incredible tower of pitas with a fabulous cinnamon sugar crust - BLOWN AWAY!The avocado toast was also phenomenal - the labneh schug was just delicious and I wanted to bottle it to take some home for later.We also got some baked goods to go and the pistachio sticky bun is living in my head rent free now.I'm so amazed by this place - I'll definitely be back to try other things on the menu!!!Plus points for clean bathrooms :)

We came here on a chilly Sunday for brunch after dropping off family at JFK. It's a very different vibe than the busy dinner scene in the best way possible. Ordered the shakshuka and salmon bagel they are both 10/10. It's my first time trying a Jerusalem bagel and I just love how thin and cripy the bagel is, it must've been bathed in the seasame heaven. My mom loved the shakashuka. Will come back for both. Our server practiced some mandarin with us which was also the cutest thing. Love this place.

Great experience. Came for brunch on a Sunday and were seated in the far back room - it was a lovely ambiance however the one negative of this was we kind of got forgotten about by our server due to being seated in the back corner. Besides that though it was a great experience we got the tuna and egg sandwiches, the beet salad and some pastries and everything was beyond delectable. Will be back!

Absolutely loved this Israeli restaurant located in Williamsburg!All the food was delicious, especially the hummus and sticky bun. The food came out fast and is perfect for sharing. The staff were kind and accommodating of our large group.Loved the ambiance and the space as well, so much natural lighting and beautifully decorated. There was lots of space on a weekday, would be a great place to get some work done as well.

This is one of those situations where the ambience is great, food is good but the service was awful. Firstly they sat us at the bar which is facing the kitchen which is loud and smoky. They were busy but I constantly felt like the server and hostess were doing me a favor when I would ask them for something. I am not sure if I would go back or not.

K'far feels like an oasis of Mediterranean sun on a gray Brooklyn day in winter.  I loved the beautiful decor, with its terracotta and cream tones, greenery, ceramics.  I finally got to try it, after salivating over photos for a long time.  The tastes were just as I imagined, in the best way.  Yemenite latte hit, with just the right combo of cinnamon, ginger and cardamom spices.   Jerusalem bagel sandwich, with flavors of roasted sesame, soft eggs, zaatar, and oozing cheese was so good, I was craving another the next day.  Pistachio sticky bun had great caramelized, nutty flavors.  Best of all, was the feeling of leisure and pleasure lingering in the air, the vibe of a languorous morning, reading the newspaper lazily over coffee; very homey, hotel vibe, as it's in the Hoxton.

Excellent service from Josh!ACYE literally on their sides, reminds me of Korean ban chan.  Their hummus is amazingly rich with excellent quality olive oil.  And omg bread, eat it right away when it's warm.Four of us got all the meats except chicken and seafood.  It was all tender, juicy, flavorful and super delicious.A rainy night had the curtains down, but we still enjoyed the view.Classy entrance in the hotel lobby to the elegance terrace like rooftop.Brooklyn in the house fo sho!

I visited this Israeli spot for lunch with a few others a while back. As others have mentioned, you're partially paying for the very nice view, and since it's a hotel restaurant. It was a bit warm at our table, and the friendly staff moved us to a cooler area. To drink, I ordered the Wolf's Fang for $18, a blend of rums, falernum, arak, passionfruit, orange, and oat milk. As described, it was their take on a milk punch, and wasn't too sweet with a bright hint of citrus. I would recommend it.The hummus was good, being thick with a strong tahini flavor, though I have preferred others. Outside of the hummus, the daily salatim included a mix of mostly pickled items. First, there was some pickled cauliflower and carrots with a bit of turmeric. The second salatim was a more unique and spicier pineapple slaw, which was refreshing, and had some herbal flavor from parsley or something similar. The third was a cabbage slaw, which was alright, as I found it a bit too lightly seasoned. The last was a mix of cucumbers, olives, and other pickles, which was standard and tasty. Alongside all of this, the pita was warm and fresh, and the staff refilled everything when we asked.For the main course, I chose the Steak Shashlik, which they spell as shishlik, for $46, which came with tahini and some pickled onions. The steak was tender and well seasoned, and the tahini added a distinctive nuttiness. I especially enjoyed making a mini sandwich with the steak, tahini, pineapple pickle, and pita. Others at the table ordered the Spicy Lamb Kofta and other shashlik options, including Chicken and Trout. In terms of sides, someone got the $15 Fries with tahina ketchup, which they enjoyed. Overall, everyone enjoyed their meal, with no one ordering dessert as a few walked over to the tasty Bertoni Gelato nearby.This place is a tad expensive, but I wouldn't mind going back. Even as a fan of pickles, I wish the salatim were a bit more varied, such as including an eggplant or bean-based mezze. I presume the dinner version is a bit more varied, as there are many more salatim, but they should put a bit more effort into the lunch one as well.

Went to Laser Wolf with my wife on one of those depressing rainy Brooklyn days where you question every life decision that led you outside. Luckily, unlimited salatim healed us emotionally.We did the prix fixe -- my wife got the steak shishlik, I got the trout. Before the actual entrees even arrived, we were already fighting for survival against wave after wave of hummus, babganush, potatoes, pickled cabbage, olives, Israeli pickles, and endless fresh pita. At one point I'm pretty sure the pita basket respawned automatically like a video game.We also ordered the halloumi which was delicious, but absolutely unnecessary. That cheese showed up like an uninvited cousin to Thanksgiving dinner. Nobody needed it, but somehow we were still happy it came.The drinks at the bar were the real MVPs. Fruity, dangerous, and exactly what you want while staring dramatically out a rainy window pretending you're in an A24 film.The trout was excellent. My wife's steak was slightly over salted, which honestly just gave us an excuse to drink more cocktails. Overall, 10/10 experience. Come hungry, leave bloated, slightly tipsy, and emotionally attached to the pita basket.

Everything was as expected and more... Loved it!The dining room is very lively (on a Thursday night) with incredible views. I was concerned about the room temp. as it was very cold outside, but with the heaters blasting and the plastic coverings, it wasn't a problem.Starting with that Salatim platter, all the food was super good and fresh. If you're not into spicy food, some of the dips on the platter might not be your thing, but we loved it. I read a review that said the hummus is the best they ever tasted, and I totally agree. The fresh pita was the perfect accompaniment--don't be shy to ask for more. And the platter is a lot of food. We were tempted to try one of the ala carte items suggested by our waiter, but glad we didn't as we couldn't finish everything.This is a really well-run restaurant. Our waiter, Richie, was super helpful, friendly, and accommodating. We were about to pass on the ice cream (lactose intolerant), but Richie offered really tasty non-dairy sorbet instead.

I love this place. Everything taste so fresh. The portions are big so share away! It's quite an arrangement of dishes.I would recommend opening up the pita and putting everything inside so you get all flavors in one bite.The server recommended the Endive and apple salad and boy did it not disappoint. It had so much flavor and just the perfect amount of spice, not overpowering. And the sauce is consisted of an apple butter and lemon vinaigrette, absolutely delicious. I was slopping it up. I really love that all the ingridents they use are infused with the Israel culture, for instance the nuts were Dukkah a Middle Eastern spice blend of nut seeds and spices with a warm toasty and nutty flavor. Absolutely delicious.Music is funky and groovy. I'd recommend coming here before 12 PM on a Sunday if you don't have a reservation as it does get pretty busy. However the server and manager were both attentive even with the whole place filled.And the best part it's on the top floor so you get some amazing views of east river and lower east side.Day or night, you'll have a fantastic view of the city that never sleep.

This popular open air Israeli kebab restaurant located atop the Hoxton Hotel in Williamsburg is a perfect venue for second spring in New York, boasting wraparound windows looking towards the Manhattan skyline. The salatim is a vegan assortment of mezze with standout baba ghanoush and fresh creamy hummus that goes perfectly with the softest warm pita I've ever had. The servers in Hawaiian shirts keep the drinks flowing and bring more complimentary pita as soon as you finish. The steak and chicken skewers are succulent and tasty. The cocktails are all good. My favorite is What's up Doc, which tasted like a well balanced ginger smoothie with carrot and apricot blended with whiskey and herbaceous genepy. The brown sugar soft serve has perfect texture with halva oat crumble and a fruit compote. A very satisfying brunch to enjoy the short lived second spring before third winter.

I had been meaning to go to Laser Wolf for so long and finally got a chance to go on a Thursday night! It's located on the rooftop of a hotel with a beautiful view of the skyline so it's probably very nice during warmer months.We each got a drink - cosmo, spicy drink, and get shishlikd, and all were delicious. The star of the show was the salatim. It came out beautifully as a spread with warm, thick pita along with a piece of paper explaining what each item was. We quickly became full from just the salatim. For the mains, we got the chicken, lamb meatballs, and eggplant. Although at first glance the portions looked small, it was actually perfect considering the salatim and dessert. For dessert, we were given the brown sugar soft serve which was actually pretty large and I didn't end up finishing it.We had so many leftovers and the meal was amazing!

What a meal and what a view, Laser Wolf was truly fantastic. A few people had told me it was their favorite restaurant in the city, and when I peeped the menu and saw just how small it was, I was a little surprised by their sentiment. But, if you only do a few things, you probably do them pretty damn near perfect, and that was the case here.We had a late lunch reservation and enjoyed their prix fixe, which is the only real option aside from some smaller side dish options. The prix fixe came with your choice of protein, hummus, pita, and the daily selection of salatim, which happened to be cucumber, olive and pickles, Baba ghanoush, and a cabbage salad/slaw. Everything was great, but we really tore through the cucumbers and cabbage. The included sides were all bottomless, so they would stop by to refill if needed.For protein we got the chicken, steak, and lamb all to share. Again, each one was fantastic. I couldn't even pick a favorite because they were all so good. I would absolutely get any of them again, and would hope to get all of them again.We also got an order of the fries, which were so good and crispy. They were pretty thick so I wasn't expecting the crispy crunch, but they were cooked wonderfully. To drink I got the Get Shishlik'd, a nice frozen cocktail that was perfect for a warm and sunny day overlooking the city.Service was very friendly and food came out pretty quickly. Servers checked in frequently to make sure we had what we needed, but never made us feel pressured to hurry.

Unlimited refills for bread AND dips???? SAY LESS.Their hummus was by far the best on their menu!!! I also really enjoyed the cabbage+fennel, roasted beets, and mushrooms+apples from the Salatim platter. The steak shishlik and spicy lamb kofta entrees were also SO delicious!! Especially with the rice and tahini sauce mmmm. And their dessert was actually really good too!

I've been to the original location in Philly, but I loved it so much that I knew I had to come here too. It's very hard to get a reservation here, but I was able to get a later dinner time for a group five. I think this is a great place to take a group of friends who have different dietary restrictions since everyone chooses their own main, but you still get to share the salatim as a group. My friend is a vegetarian, and they had plenty of options for her to choose from.I loved trying all of the salatim with my friends; it's really hard to not fill up before the entrees come! It's already a ton of food, but I really recommend getting the french fries. They're not your normal fries. They're super thick but crispy and perfectly seasoned. The only complaint I have about the place is that we were seated at the last table at the end so the heater didn't reach us. It was a freezing night, so we all had to keep our winter coats on. I wish they had a portable heat lamp next to our table instead of on the wall. The ice cream at the end was delicious, but we were all so cold that no one finished it.

Laser Wolf is a must try. The Pre-fixe is an amazing deal and the food is fantastic. We started with the Salatim Hummus. Every dip and salad was delicious. They give unlimited refills of any salad or dip you want and pita. We then had the Aged T-bone steak for 2. It was perfectly cooked and a huge amount of food. We finished with the brown butter ice cream which is one of my favorite desserts ever. Our waitress was so attentive and nice. And.. the view! It was beautiful.
View is great.  It's at the 11th floor of a hotel. Drinks are good too.  Get the Branzino and the brisket.  Cosmo was good as well as the guava frozen drink.  Staff is great.  Happy Yelping!

Really cool location, and worth a visit for the ambiance and unique dining experience!I really liked the concept of the salatim, my favorites being the dips and hummus. One of the best hummus' I've had in the city! For the mains, we tried the lamb kofta, beef and lamb kofta, ocean trout, and grilled eggplant. I unfortunately found the trout to be lacking in flavor and overall an odd texture. My favorite dish was probably the beef and lamb kofta, as it was well seasoned and balanced well with the tahini sauce.Not sure I'd come back again unless for a special occasion. But be sure to come hungry!

Laser Wolf is an Israeli rooftop restaurant located in The Hoxton. Everything is cooked on a charcoal grill with views over the East River.This spot has quickly become my favorite restaurant right now. We went on a cold December evening and everyone was bundled up in full winter gear, which honestly added to the experience. It's definitely chilly, but the heaters during the winter months make it manageable (and worth it tbh).The unlimited salatim was fantastic and easily one of the highlights. Everything was impossible to stop eating. The steak shishlik and brisket kebab were unbelievable. And the French fries with tehina ketchup were the crunchiest, most perfect fries I've ever had.The summer months are definitely an ideal time to dine here, but it's truly worth going whenever you can.

Unlimited salatim, great options for meat and fish lovers, and delicious brown sugar ice cream to end the meal. People are in and out of the restaurant up until 11pm and later. Go to Jaffa cocktail bar (located in the same hotel) for a drink before dinner.

This Israeli restaurant  is located on the rooftop of the The Hoxton Hotel, with all seating outdoors. I'd recommend making a reservationso you can enjoy your meal while taking in the stunning Manhattan night views.We didn't book ahead and ended up waiting over an hour. We were seated at the bar, facing away from the skyline.The menu is prix fixe: each person selects one main course, which comes with pita, hummus, and about ten small dishes as starters.

What a treat coming here the food was amazing , such a selection of goodies..The tray presented at the beginning with all these different type of delicacies to go with your main dish selection .We picked the brisket and the lamb dish and also the grilled fish dish .Food is just amazing and dessert included .

Great quality food and beautiful views. Went in the winter so imagine views better as it gets warmer. Large area and good temp despite the email notice. Went with friends for Prefix - meat and small bites were all delicious as well as ice cream. Overall was soo good- great service, atmosphere, and food. Seasoning was delicious and recommend

Underwhelmed by the quality of the food, specifically the meats. Steak felt overcooked, hard to chew, kinda bland, did not feel medium rare. Spicy lamb Krofta tasted overly salty... not spicy at all. Portion size was depressing, when both came out, I thought it was just one portion....Unlimited sides and pita bread kinda make up for it. At least I didn't leave feeling hungry and not satiated on top of the subpar meats. The hummus and baba ghanoush were really good. Soft serve ice cream was bomb as well.Overhyped spot, but the views are pretty great, especially if you can get a window seat. Ambiance is immaculate and super cute

My favorite part about Laser Wolf was the view! It's on a rooftop with a view of the beautiful city skyline. The menu is pretty simple which makes it easy to choose. It's a pre fixe menu where you choose your main (meats, fish, vegetables, etc) and it comes with salatim and dessert. The server will explain all the different dishes in the salatim. It was so fun trying all the dips and vegetables in the salatim and their unlimited refills. It's also served with pita bread. We got the chicken and spicy lamb and both were good. My favorite was the chicken since it was so tender. For dessert we got a brown sugar apple and shortbread ice cream which was delicious!

Came here for anniversary dinner. Cool rooftop views and the hotel entrance is great/ you ride the elevator up. Did the prix fixe. Mezzes were delicious with so many selections. I didn't know that I would even like babaganoush but it was great. They gave us extra pita.

This place is Amazing! Great views as well as we got seated on the rooftop. Hack: wait in line right when they open if you don't have a reservation, otherwise you need to book way in advance.They  provide unlimited "appetizers" with the purchase of an entree. The entree did take quite a while to come out, but we were kept full by all the refillable appetizers. The blueberry ice cream at the end of dinner was so delicious and paired nicely with the texture of the crumble crunch. Highly recommend trying this place, but be prepare to spend a little more

We had a wonderful experience for lunch at Laser Wolf. Everything we tried was delicious, service was great, and the views from the rooftop were incredible! We felt it'd be a bit pricy to do a prix fixe, but were able to build our own delicious meal at a reasonable cost. Would definitely return!

Maybe it's because I just came back from mezze heaven in the meyhanes of Istanbul or because August 2025 feels like a peculiar time for an opulent feast at a Levantine restaurant - but the impeccable views from last year's hottest restaurant didn't save my experience at Laser Wolf from mediocrity.Like many other foodies, I came here primarily for the salatim platter, a decadent thali of mezzes that begins the prix-fixe meal. My favorite items were the pickles, including Turkish style turşu and Palestinian olives. The hummus and babaganoush were both unmemorable, and the eggplant was spicy without being flavorful.Surprisingly, my main dish - a mushroom kebab special - ended up being my preferred part of the evening. It had a lovely crunch to it and was served with a decadent combination of tahini and deep-fried garlic. If I were to return, I would probably order this a la carte if available.

The location of Laser Wolf is absolutely stunning the panoramic rooftop view alone makes the visit feel special. We started off with the salatim, of course. The hummus was really good, super creamy and flavorful. As for the other dips and appetizers, they were definitely more of an acquired taste. Some flavors were just a little confusing for me certain dishes were unexpectedly spicy and didn't really need to be. The cucumbers were fresh and delicious, but the cold potatoes kind of threw me off. While the unlimited refills of the salatim were a nice touch, I honestly would've been just as happy with warm pita and hummus alone.I got the chicken shishlik as my main, which was tasty juicy and well-seasoned but not particularly memorable. It was good, just not something I'd rave about.For dessert, we had the soft serve, which was definitely a highlight. The sesame flavor was another acquired taste, but I actually liked it. It was unique and worked well with the sweetness.Overall, I think I expected a bit more based on the hype. Maybe the flavor profile just isn't quite my style. It was a cool experience and I'm glad I tried it, but I'm not sure I'd go back again.

One of the best meals ever. Mediterranean is my favorite cuisine and this place was my favorite by far!We made sure to order as much as we possibly could stuff ourselves with. Each course was delicious and everything was packed with flavor.It had a great interior too and was a great place to go for a dinner date. I'd definitely go back!

I absolutely loved the food here, and the views from the rooftop are gorgeous. All of the dishes were really well-executed. It's expensive ($60 with tax and tip each with a vegetarian entree), but good value with the unlimited salatim.SALATIMHummus + Pita - the hummus is super rich and creamy, and the pita was fluffy with almost nutty wheat flavor.Babaganoush - subtle smokiness balanced with creaminess.Turkish Summer Squash - relatively light and fresh with a savory tomato sauce.Lutenitsa - a nice tomato sauce type item.Israeli Pickles - bright and herbal. The olives had a subtle bitterness that contrasted well with the other items.Pineapple w/ Celery - probably my favorite single item for how unique it was. The tartness and sweetness from the tropical fruit played nicely with the dill.Yemenite Pickles - crispy and subtle tang.Cabbage  Fennel w/ Schug - fresh and crunchy with nice herb flavor.Spicy Cucumbers w/ Harif -Tunisian Potatoes - the potatoes were extra tasty with the skin on, and I loved the mix of spices.Beets w/ Pickled Fresno Peppers - slightly sweet and sour, with the golden beets cooked to a perfectly soft texture.ENTREEGrilled Eggplant - buttery soft with some tartness from the tomato and pickled toppings, and a good blend of herbs and spices.Shawarma Spiced Cauliflower - my favorite of the two. The cauliflower sits on top of a creamy base of labneh. The cauliflower itself is nicely charred without getting overly soft, and is well-seasoned. The pine nuts over the top are a great finishing touch.DESSERTBrown sugar soft serve - not too sweet, and comes with delicious crispy and fruity toppings.

Wonderful foodUnique menuFixed : each table gets round platter with 11 tasty and surprisingly light Middle Eastern appetizers, then you get to select an entree and then dessert.All was superbPleasant atmosphere, overlooks East river and very pleasant service

From the food to the ambiance, this is really a great experience. Laser Wolf is great for both meat eaters and vegetarians to share food because the main salatim is literally all vegetables (but DELICIOUS) and you get to pick your own grilled meat/veggie.The salatim and bread is unlimited but the food was so filling, I did not need extras. The salatim flavor profile was so diverse, some being spicy, some pickly, it was great to experiment!We also got the fries which were seasoned well and crispy, and it did not need any sauce. Me and my friend got the steak and chicken shishlik and both were good portions and seasoned well! I usually prefer steak but honestly could not tell you which of the chicken and steak I preferred.My other friend got the cauliflower, and this was just as good as the meat as the cauliflower soaked up all the flavor. In the end, we got soft serve and it was perfect end to our meal.No complaints to service and eating at this place during sunset was so beautiful with the view of the water. I would say reservations for sunset need to be booked out when reservations come out or you will be stuck with a 10 PM reservation.

We absolutely love Laser Wolf!  The food is fantastic and if you're dining for 2, I love their approach of mezze-sampling-with-a-main that allows you to try so many delicious things.  Their bread is fantastic and their mains (I've tried chicken, lamb and beef mains) are all really good.The vibe is also really cool on the rooftop of a nice hotel with ridiculous sunset views of Manhattan.  Reservations are hard!  A pro-tip if you don't care about having a table with a constant view of the skyline, you can show up and try to get bar seats.  We've had very good luck getting bar seats (that face the bar, back to views), and the food is obviously just as good, and to be honest we usually prefer bartender service :)This is a can't-miss if you're in Williamsburg.

Great food, very tasty dishes. Unlimited side dishes (pita bread, hummus hummus and others)Portion size is a bit on the smaller end for entrees, but starters were good portions (unlimited as well)Service was quick and friendly. They took time to explain each dish and what was in itSeating is on the rooftop, on a hot summer day, they tarped off the rooftop for air conditioned environment

This place has been on my list for a while, after being sent a YouTube video about their cocktail program.The first time I tried to get a reservation, some time ago, was painful.  I am often a single diner, and many apps won't let you reserve for one.  When I emailed, I was met with resistance to get a seat anywhere at the bar, and finally gave up.This time, I randomly walked over from my hotel, at around 7 PM on a Tuesday night, and the place wasn't crowded at all.  The hostess was warm and sat me at a regular table immediately.Servers seem to work in teams here.  Everyone was very attentive and helpful, and explained the menu well.  And if you're not familiar with this type of dining concept, you will require some explanation.I started with the chips and salsa, which came with a flight of five salsas, all of varying spices and flavor profiles.  Each one was unique and tasted great in its own way.  I also had the papayas bravas, which came in a huge portion.  It was pretty typical tasting and didn't really stand out.Other appetizers/smaller dishes include cheese quesadillas, chips and guacamole, Cesar salad, and tacos.  They have a taco of the day and taco de coliflor.They also have some smaller dishes that can be mixed and matched, especially if you're dining in a group, including tostadas (yellowfin tuna, smoked yellowtail, scallops) and aguachiles (heat levels of verde, Amarillo, negro).  Aguachile is essentially ceviche.  Ensenada has what looks like an impressive variety, made with cucumber, radish, avocado, various types of chiles, onion, and spices.  Alas, I did not try these because I am not a fan of raw fish.  Sorry, not sorry.They also have larger entrees, including a fish of the day (Branzino the day I went), octopus and chorizo (I also don't eat octopus), and pork rib milanesa, which is what I ended up going with, at the recommendation of my server.I pictured something different when I ordered the pork rib milanesa--I was too dumb to realize milanesa is a South American dish of pounded, breaded, and fried cutlet of meat.  While it wasn't my bag overall, it was cooked well and had a sauce that added complexity to the flavors.  It was a good dish, just not my thing.They have a huge, impressive mezcal/tequila list as well as cocktails.  I had the su primo (strawberry infused primo apertivo and topo chico), the abolito (tequila, mezcal, avocado, tomatillo, lime), and fin de semana (mezcal, macerated pineapple, angostura bitters, and lemon).  I also followed up with their shot of the day and some key lime pie.The cocktails were smooth, not overpowering, easy to drink, and tasted great.  The flavors were complex and varied, from sweeter to more savory.  The mezcal list is HUGE.  You can also get flights to try.  The keylime pie was fine, but next time I would just stick to liquid desert.

I've heard great things about Ensenada and finally got around to trying it.I would definitely get a reservation because walk in attempts have been ~2 hours both times I tried.I tried the patatas bravas and aguachiles and they were solid, though nothing thay made me too excited.Star of the show is the fish of the day. My friend told me to add pineapple butter. I would come back for the fish alone!

Made a same-day reservation and had an excellent dinner here. My boyfriend and I ordered:+ La manzanita cocktail+ Fin de semana cocktail+ Green garden cocktail+ Ensalada Cesar+ aguachile pescado verde (small)+ tostada de atun "al pastor"+ Tostada de vieira (scallop)+ Fried fish tacos+ Patatas bravas+ Hibiscus pieThe highlights were the aguachile, fish tacos and hibiscus pie. Also really liked the salad and scallop tostada. My favorite cocktail was the La manzanita. Ingredients are very high quality and on par with spots like Oxomoco.My only note is that I wish they had more side carb options - it's a lot of raw seafood + chips/tostadas unless you get a big main (we wanted to try a lot of things so we skipped the bigger dishes), and I was still pretty hungry until we added on an order of potatoes. Next time will definitely get a main!

Another amazing visit. The shrimp tacos, scallop tostada, salsas, and al pastor branzino were all perfect. I'd probably do an aguachile instead of the fish next time though, unless I was going with a bigger group. (This was a ton of food.) The key lime pie was excellent, but we were less into the chocolate mousse.

This was outstanding. The salsas were all fairly different than you get at other places, and all excellent. The shrimp taco, fluke tostada, escabeche ceviche, negro aguachile, and soft shell crab were all perfect, as well. I wish we could've tried the whole menu.I was also impressed with the service -- my wife hates cilantro and every food runner commented on how cilantro was left off because of it.Can't wait to come back.

We decided to try this tapas restaurant in Williamsberg, Brooklyn. The restaurant had a two hour limit and ironically the service was a little slow.We got a margarita and a supremo cocktail which were strong and refreshing. We ordered the bravas which were crispy on the outside and melt-in-your mouth on the inside. Definitely a good  amount for two people.The chips seemed freshly made and the all but one of the salsas were delicious. The soy sauce and sesame oil dip didn't seem to go well with the chips. There also wasn't a lot of chips for the amount of salsa provided.We also ordered the scallop tostada was perfect. The flavor was light and texture was nice.Overall, the food was great and I would definitely recommend the restaurant.

I always pass by here and this has been on my list for ages. I finally decided to give it a try and it didn't disappoint.So i actually was a solo walk in. The hostess was very kind and friendly and they happy was able to accommodate a spot for me. The music was vibes and it was as if they had borrowed by playlist.Ensenada is about mezcal. Im a mezcal girly and the list was extensive ! The drink i got excuse me the crafted cocktail i have with the cantaloupe ball was tasty. The food was 10/10 and you can't go wrong with what you choose. I will be back because i need to try the whole menu lol.
Big fan. Definitely get the fish of the day, the aguachiles, and the hibiscus pie. Very yummy!

Came for dinner, really cute inside with a triangle layout which was interesting! Happy hour was until 7p I think, their margs were super strong, AND spicy if you get it spicy. I ended up going back to regular because it was too much for me personally but it was a nice kick. The food was also quite nice, especially with the different salsas with the chips. Shrimp tacos were good, I didn't really enjoy the cauliflower, it seemed not too flavorful for me.

Ensenada is a vibey Mexican seafood spot in South Williamsburg that partially turns into a club at night. The menus highlight mezcal and seafood, two of my favorite things. Though we came here as a party of two and still had a great time, I'd say Ensenada is more of a group spot, as I noticed there were more large parties than small ones. Service is excellent, food is paced well, no notes there. Overall, a really fun evening with delicious food and drink, with the added bonus of basement jams and dancing after hours.We came here at around 8:45 on a Saturday night. We had some train issues on the way and texted the restaurant, and they were kind enough to shift our once-earlier reservation time. The space itself is fairly small, with larger tables lining the perimeter of the space and a few high-top tables for pairs in the center. There's also some seating at the majestic bar, which is designed and lit beautifully. We were at one of the high-top tables which was initially concerning because we didn't know how the table could fit our many courses, but they paced out our dishes such that we didn't have any issues.As a mezcal lover, I was overwhelmed with excitement when I saw the cocktail menu. I started with the El Casino (condesa gin, lillet, mal bien zacate limon, guindilla pepper), which is an expertly crafted martini/vesper variation that's so smooth it's dangerous. Highly recommend if you're even sort of into martinis. My other go-to aside from a martini is a negroni, so I had to try the Mezcal White Negroni (mezcal espadin, lillet, suze, lemon), which was also delicious. My husband got the Seasonal Paloma with mezcal and then the Dios Mio (hoja santa infused mezcal, dark rum, cointreau, avocado pit orgeat, lemon, basil oil), both of which were delicious but the second was a true masterpiece of a drink.Food-wise, we decided to get a good sampling of the menu, though with two of us were not able to get the famous whole fish, as it would've meant sacrificing some of the other dishes. Overall, the food is and bright, with clean and bold flavors and plenty of spice. Eats, below:Tostada de atun w/ guacamole, salsa macha, plum sesame seeds: A+Hot out of the gate was this seriously delicious and beautifully presented tostada, our favorite dish of the night. Just a perfect and complete bite with all the right textures and flavors.Ceviche Tradicional w/ pescado: A-This was very good just less exciting than the other dishes. We had ordered so much fish that after a while we just became less interested in this than the other stuff, but it was still enjoyable.Ensalada Cesar w/ chicories, white anchovy dressing, grapefruit, parmesan, sourdough breadcrumbs: AWe felt like we needed some veggies so we opted for the one veggie dish on the menu (other than the cucumber aguachile). We thoroughly enjoyed it and gobbled it up quickly. I love a Cesar with seasonal produce. I'm not usually a big fan of citrus in general (just doesn't do it for me) but for whatever reason I loved the grapefruit in this salad.Aguachile amarillo w/ camaron (shrimp), aji amarillo, turmeric, lemongrass, cucumber, radish, avocado: AThis one was the medium spicy option of the aguachiles, a little too spicy for my husband but he was able to enjoy some of it. The flavor combo here is one of my favorite savory things and it paired nicely with the shrimp.Taco de pescado w/ chipotle aioli, slaw, pico de gallo: A+Granted I only had one small bite of this because it was just an order of one small taco for my husband, but that one bite was pretty magical. Definitely one of the best fish tacos I've ever had. Few things are more satisfying than a fish taco with chipotle aioli.

My family came here to celebrate my sister's birthday. We ordered the chips and guacamole, two tacos de pescado, a taco de camaron, tostada de viera, camaron, ceviche, verde aguachile, negro aguachile, the jaiba suave, the housemade pickles, the hibiscus pie, and the crema de limon. Everything was really good. I didn't know what to expect because I haven't seen a Mexican restaurant with a menu quite like this one. I was pleasantly surprised.The food was really good, and I noticed that they have outstanding press coverage. I would definitely come here again. I would also want to order more of the tacos in the future. The restaurant was pretty good and overall I think this is a great place for when you want to gather with friends or family for a special event or just because.

This review is admittedly from the perspective of a vegetarian at a seafood-heavy establishment, so do take it with a grain of salt.We all started with the chips with guac and salsa. The salsa thali, for lack of a better word, was probably my favorite item of the evening. There was a very wide gamut of salsa flavors, including a particular delicious arbol chili. I wouldn't have minded a more fulsome portion of many of these varieties.For mains, the vegetarians at the table shared the cucumber aguachile and the cauliflower tacos. The salsa verde for the aguachile was very fragrant, but I couldn't help but feel that a meatier vegetable, like an artichoke or heart of palm, would do a better job of absorbing the sauce. The cauliflower tacos had a nice lightly-battered texture, but some of the aforementioned salsas would perhaps have brought more flavor here than the light schmear of chipotle aioli.

Everything was tasty, seafood-heavy menu. Some interesting flavor combos. The standouts were the tostadas (esp the tuna/pineapple). The restaurant was a bit dark and loud for us. Friendly service. Nice night out, good to try, not sure we would go back.

Is this a joke? Am I on candid camera? $16 for chips and salsa is insane! ESPECIALLY when the chips aren't even homemade! The food here is good, I have to say, but not for the prices. $16 for TWO tacos! A cheese quesadilla for $15!!  I would give this place a lot more stars if they weren't so expensive. Mexican food doesn't need to cost that much.

This place is fantastic! Had 8:15 reservations and the first impression was sexy atmosphere. Got the high table, they were ok, I think these tables would be great for Happy Hour, but I would reserve a table next time for a full dinner. They have many great happy hour deals I want to take advantage of these in the near future.Anyways started with a refreshing cantaloupe cocktail. Next had the Scallop tostada, mix seafood ceviche, and the aguachile nigro. All three were delicious, all very fresh and super flavorful! The aguachile was really spicy, maybe a bit too much for me, but it was so delicious I couldn't stop eating it. Luckily it comes with corn chips which are good to the quench the burn. For our entree we shared the Pescado del dia "al pibil", the whole fish. This fish was outstanding, if you come here just get this and the pineapple butter. The butter was like "crack"! Decided to try the Hibiscus pie. When I got a spoon full of the pie with the crunchies and the chocolate ganache all at the same bite it was heavenly! Can't wait to come back here, 5 Stars!!!

We went here because it came highly recommended on sites like the infatuation, etc.. what a mistake. The salsa platter includes a chili crisp (?) which does not go with tortilla chips at all. The Oaxaca cheese in the ($15!) quesadilla was such a poor, flavorless choice -- especially with the store bought tasting tortilla. And don't even get me started on the margaritas. I've never experienced a "Mexican" restaurant that rims their glasses with table salt? Overpriced and overhyped online.
