Yuuuuum!! We went a month ago and I keep wanting to go back. The last time I loved seafood dishes this much is when were at Entremar in Mexico City. The two tostadas on the menu are a must! The tuna had roasted sesame, umami flavors and the scallop tostada had a zesty, citrus pop that accompanied the tuna so well. We also loved the chips, salsa flight and guacamole. My husband os obsessed with the crispy fish taco - says its the best he has ever had. Everything else we ate was 10/10. We had a group of 6 and all of us left stuffed and sooo happy.Special and necessary shout out to the dios mio mezcal cocktail. It was perfect.I want one now .
amazing service, tasteful food, drinks are amazing  and very nice ambience. I will definitely come again

Great New Mexican seafood restaurant on a bright corner in South Williamsburg.Start with the Tuna Tostadas ($19 for 2) and definitely try some tacos, made with tortillas from For All Things Good. For something bigger, get the excellent Pescado Al Pastor ($43) and make yourself some more tacos. The Hibiscus Pie ($14) for dessert is great as well.

Very lowkey spot with nice dim lighting. The tostadas were my favoriteeeeeee! The aguachiles were a bit too spicy for me but still so very good. And the pescado del día was delicioussss, I don't often eat fish and this was amazing. I would definitely go again, and try out the rest of the menu! Service was excellent.

Whoa this place blew me away!! The fish we had was so fresh and delicious, could have been at a sushi restaurant quality wise but then flavor wise was so unique and had a good amount of spice but not too much and was creative! We had the fish tacos too which were a great portion size and came out hot and fresh! The restaurant itself is really nice and cute! It's a great vibe you can dress nicely and fit it but also don't have to go over the top. Love love loved it here

wishing i could rate this 3.5 stars. The food itself was pretty great. Aguachiles, moles, cocktails, and even guac all stood out as great dishes. I would recommend this place to anyone looking for actually good Mexican food in NY. this is the only place i've been to where i'm actually met with proper heat/spice. I think prices are a little steep-- i know 2 others that have spent around $90 a person which seems like too much. service wasn't the best, but the food is 100% worth visiting.

Reading the other reviews I am tempted to believe there was some problem the night I dined here. I came with two friends visiting from LA because I'd been wanting to try it and we are all Mexican food lovers. The chips and salsa and aguachiles starters were great, as were the drinks and service. Two of us ordered the fried fish tacos and the third ordered the steak with molé sauce. The fish in the fish tacos was so salty that the other flavors were completely overpowered and you could only taste salt. My friend said his were the same and neither of us finished them.  Tbh, these were the worst fish tacos I have ever had and I have eaten many, including in Ensenada where I traveled frequently when I lived in CA.  The steak was a decent 6.5/10. The molé sauce was underwhelming and also very salty. I really wanted to like this place, and again I'm not sure if there was some issue in the kitchen the night we ate there given that so many rave about the food, but I was very disappointed.

Oh my WOW. This meal was INCREDIBLE.Did lots of research beforehand and then chatted with other patrons at the entrance and our waitress extensively to get all of the best recommendations and we were so happy we followed them all  :)You absolutely MUST get the tuna tostadas and the pescado del día "al pastor"! Everything was awesome, but those 2 were my favorites! I could eat 7 of those tostadas and just eat the pineapple relish on the pescado with a spoon (and I might have...)!One note - the margarita was SUPER spicy as well as a lot of the food so err on the side of ordering lower spice levels than your usual. We got the mildest aguachiles at the waitress' recommendation and we're still having runny noses (and we like some spice).Also GET THE CHIPS, GUAC, AND SALSA!!! They were eaten up way too quickly for me to get pictures, but probably the best chips, guac, and salsa (6 different kinds and all delicious) I have ever had.Cannot recommend this place enough! The food plus the excellent service and vibes make it a 10/10 for me!

The cauliflower tacos were really good and flavorful!! I also enjoyed the spicy margarita. We had good service and enjoyed the atmosphere. Just know that happy hour only applies to the bar and high top tables. We didn't know this and it was too busy to move tables so we weren't able to get the deal.
The service. The bus boys kept hovering, the waitress asked to remove plates we weren't done with, TWICE. Honestly, the food was decent but not worth the feeling you're not wanted there.
We went for a family birthday and in our party we have a gluten free pescatarian and a vegetarian and everyone found something they were happy with. The fish tacos were very good. Would go again.

augachiles- was told to order to smalls. don't do itaugustine negro. - smoky fun, lingering heat begging for next bitemiddle spice- watered and a bit too sourspoon to mouth then bite chiptortillas a bit overcooked and crunchypork. tasty with figs and peppers but at time more breading than meat.key lime pie is deconstructed winner. would get anotherservice is mid. servers put drink tray over phone. they are not very serious. had to ask multiple questions about the menu. bartender forgot drink order.

We walked in to Ensenada Friday night and grabbed the sole remaining outdoor table. Not sure if it was an out of sight out of mind situation with the outdoor tables but unfortunately service was verrry very slow. From what I could tell, it seemed like the move here was chips and guac and salsa and some shots of mezcal... but we went a different route and tried some cocktails, along with shrimp and fish tacos, vuelve a la vida campechano and the pork milanesa. The food was all pretty solid in terms of flavor, but the portions were a bit small. The mezcal Negroni was a good choice. They sent us home with the limón curd dessert because of the service/food delays which was okay - the kiwi was a nice touch.All that to say, while the food was tasty, it unfortunately wasn't worth the wait.

I'd rate Ensenada for and a half stars.Food and mezcal selection was outstanding but the portion size left a bit to be desired.Upon entry, you'll notice that there is a large enclosure which is the entrance to the Black Flamingo - the speakeasy under Ensenada. They have an excellent selection of mezcals. It's a bit overwhelming but they have a superb bartender to help make recommendations. I was thrilled with all of the mezcals that he recommend.Food wise, we started with the fish and scallop tostados. I sensed a strong East Asian influence with the tostados. They had a chili sauce which added spiciness and richness. Both tostados were great.Next we had the Caesar salad. I suspect the dressing was house made as it had a distinct anchovy flavor. Lettuce was crispy and there loss of nutty elements to the dish.After we shared the traditional campecheon ceviche which was outstanding. Super rich and delicious. Probably my favorite dish of the evening. The following items for us was the shrimp tacos. Quite flavorful with a pleasant bitterness to them.We finished our meal of with a slice of hibiscus pie for dessert which had a cookie crust. It was sprinkled with sea salt as well as nuts and had a heaping of what I thought was chocolate ganache. Another flavorful dish. Lots of richness and saltiness.Service was super helpful and attentive. They timed the dishes perfectly for us and made sure there was always food at our table without overcrowding it.

Lemme first say I hate leaving bad reviews but I'm almost embarrassed because this place was my idea. I wanted to come here so badly and my friends and I had the worst experience we've had in the longest time . I'll say the margarita was perfect . The aguachiles were fine ? But definitely for the basics palate which I kinda knew was gonna end up happening which is annoying . The fish is supposed to be barely cooked in the acid these were fully cooked thru . It was ceviche . Which is so lame . And boring . And not authentic . Cool .Anyway not the major issue obviously that's not reason enough to get one star it's the service the kitchen the mindblowingly bad experience with it ?? First of all we waited at the bar for awhile for a table to get up from dessert because he said we probably don't wanna sit at the front table near the door . Correct . So no problem we had drinks at the bar . They did stay a long time though but when about to leave he said they'd have to sit us up front anyway now? Maybe cuz that was a 4 top and we were 3 but extremely rude to have us wait (with a reservation) for a table and then take it back from us??? Never had that happen before .Anyway we sit up front : said he put a heater on for us . Didn't . Wasn't even plugged in . Cool . We order everything together . She asks if we have a food allergy and my friend says she has a shrimp allergy so she won't be eating the aguachile obviously .Oh ps : at this point of ordering at 9 pm on a Wednesday there are only 5 tables all on the same side of the restaurant as us . So the table we waited for empty??? Including every other table in the middle and on that same side . Why did we wait for no reason and why did we still get seated at the shit table ? No one will ever know .Anyway back to allergy so we order everything at the same time . Salad comes immediately naturally . Aguachiles a couple mins later . And then nothing . No one even comes over . There are 5 tables in the restaurant and two servers working . 50 mins go by . And can I say onceMore all three of us are long time bartenders . I don't complain about shit unless it's warranted (and yes we still left a 20% tip cuz that's what we do ) so my friend goes upAnd says it's been 50 minutes and no other food (we just got tacos) and we ordered everything at the same time and told you our friend had an allergy so you'd assume she'd push her food out as well? And instead of apologizing she gave her an attitude and said "the kitchen puts the food out as it's ready" ok that literally defeats the reason the other food is delayed ??!? So you're saying you put all the food in at the same time ??? And that the tacos just took that long to come ???? Lies . Or what like what? I really can not wrap my brain around it . Tacos in a slow restaurant don't take 50 minutes . And by that I mean60 . We told them to cancel we had to go . And she said they're almost done ??? OVER AN HOUR ???So no. By principle we can't and also we just can't wait either way we had a movie which we got there 2 hours before it LOL .They took the tacos off of course and that was it . And it was an attitude across the whole way that we cancelled .The pretentiousness and blatant lies were shocking :So my friend ate a third of a salad . We ate the same plus ceviche not aguachiles. And then had to order a meal at nitehawk instead .Thanks for the worst dining experience we've all had since at least before the pandemic . And probably a lot longer since I do not write reviews but this one deserved . Sorry
